user_query:
I'm a retired nurse from Norfolk helping coordinate our volunteer liaison meeting notes. Please use the files under input/ to compile clean minutes and an action tracker, and make this reproducible via a command-line script.

Requirements:
1) Create and run a command-line script at scripts/compile_minutes.py that reads:
   - input/agenda_email.txt (meeting metadata and agenda)
   - input/attendees.csv (names, roles, and attendance status)
   - input/raw_notes.md (scratch notes with decisions and action items)
   - input/resource.html (saved web content with flu clinic dates and mask guidance)
   and writes all outputs to the output/ folder.

2) Extract structured data from the inputs and produce all of the following artifacts:
   a) output/meeting_minutes.md that includes at minimum these sections populated from the inputs:
      - Title (from agenda_email.txt)
      - Date and Time (from agenda_email.txt)
      - Location (from agenda_email.txt)
      - Chair (from agenda_email.txt)
      - Attendees (names and roles with status=Attending from attendees.csv)
      - Apologies (names and roles with status=Apologies from attendees.csv)
      - Agenda (ordered list from agenda_email.txt)
      - Decisions (all lines starting with "Decision:" in raw_notes.md, grouped under the agenda item they appear beneath)
      - Action items (a list that references each Action ID from raw_notes.md)
      - Appendix: Flu clinic dates (a small table extracted from the table in resource.html)
      - Appendix: Mask guidance (the guidance sentence from resource.html)
   b) output/action_items.csv with columns exactly: id, description, owner, due_date (YYYY-MM-DD), priority (blank if not present), agenda_item, owner_status. Parse all lines in raw_notes.md that begin with "Action [A-" and capture:
      - id: the A-### token
      - description: the task description text (without the "Action [A-###]:" prefix)
      - owner: the assigned person’s name (from inline "Owner: Name" or the person named immediately after the colon if present)
      - due_date: a date in YYYY-MM-DD if present; leave blank if none
      - priority: if "Priority: <value>" appears on the line, capture it; else blank
      - agenda_item: the nearest agenda section heading under which the action appears
      - owner_status: cross-reference attendees.csv by exact name (case-insensitive trim); set to Attending, Apologies, or Unlisted accordingly
   c) output/extracted.json containing a single JSON object with keys:
      - meeting: {title, date, time, location, chair}
      - agenda: array of agenda item strings in order
      - attendees: array of {name, role, status}
      - decisions: array of {agenda_item, text}
      - actions: array of {id, description, owner, due_date, priority, agenda_item, owner_status}
      - flu_clinics: array of {town, date, venue} extracted from the HTML table
      - mask_guidance: the guidance sentence from resource.html

3) The script must be re-runnable to regenerate outputs from the inputs. Provide a CLI that works like:
   python scripts/compile_minutes.py --agenda input/agenda_email.txt --attendees input/attendees.csv --notes input/raw_notes.md --web input/resource.html --outdir output

4) Do not hardcode values; all content in the outputs must be derived from the provided input files. Keep formatting reasonable, but only the specified sections/columns/keys are required for verification.

Deliverables to produce under the workspace:
- scripts/compile_minutes.py (runnable)
- output/meeting_minutes.md
- output/action_items.csv
- output/extracted.json All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
